item.text: I’m going to read the assignment first, then inspect the `visual/` app structure to identify where to add tests and implementation with TDD before running the full mocha suite.

item.aggregated_output:
# Scenario Builder Assignment

## Master Visual Gap Inventory (from visual audit)
- No automated tests for `visual/js/*`, `visual/index.html`, or UI interactions.
- High-risk uncovered areas: controller state transitions, button state mapping, operation replay timing, panel parser wiring, view dirty-node reset, DOM draw/erase/drag flows.
- Known potential defects to cover: IDA* heuristic selector mismatch in `Panel.getFinder`, unreachable `modified` transition usage in controller, CSS selector typo `#hide_instruction:hover` vs `#hide_instructions`.

## Your Scope
Own visual/scenario tests and integration harness for demo behaviors.

### Gaps to cover (specific files/functions/edge cases)
1. `visual/js/controller.js`
- State machine contract tests: legal/illegal transitions and side effects.
- Button label/disabled/callback mapping for `ready`, `searching`, `paused`, `finished`, `modified`.
- `hookPathFinding`, `step`, `loop`, `clearOperations`, finish-on-queue-depletion behavior.
- Mouse interaction scenarios for drag/draw/erase flows.

2. `visual/js/panel.js`
- Table-driven parser tests by accordion selection -> finder class + options.
- Regression test for IDA* heuristic selector using wrong radio group.
- Input sanitization tests (`weight >= 1`, IDA `timeLimit` fallback `-1`, diagonal/JPS options).

3. `visual/js/view.js`
- Unit tests for `generateGrid`, coordinate converters, dirty tracking, blocked overlays, footprint clearing.
- `buildSvgPath`, draw/clear path behavior, `showStats` formatting.

4. Boot and compatibility scenarios
- `visual/js/main.js`: verify `Panel.init()` before `Controller.init()`.
- `Raphael.svg=false` redirects to `visual/notsupported.html`; `Raphael.svg=true` initializes normally.
- Include lightweight DOM integration scenarios for controls (start/pause/resume/restart/cancel/clear).

5. Style-level regression check
- Add a focused test or static assertion catching selector mismatch for hide-instructions hover rule.

## Where to put new tests
- `test/visual/controller.spec.js`
- `test/visual/panel.spec.js`
- `test/visual/view.spec.js`
- `test/visual/main-and-dom-integration.spec.js`

## TDD Rule (required)
Write failing tests first, then implement. Verify existing tests still pass.

## Verify command
`npx mocha --require should test/**/*.js`

item.text: Assignment has substantial scope across `controller.js`, `panel.js`, `view.js`, and boot/integration flows. Next I’ll inspect current `visual/` code plus existing tests to design targeted failing specs before any implementation edits.

item.text: I’ve mapped the current implementation and confirmed the likely IDA heuristic bug in `panel.js`. I’ll now add the new `test/visual/*.spec.js` files with failing tests around these behaviors, then patch app code until the suite passes.

item.text: I’m now creating the new `test/visual` specs with isolated VM-based stubs for browser globals so we can test controller/panel/view/main behavior under Node. I’ll intentionally assert the known regressions (IDA heuristic selector and hover selector) so the first run fails before fixes.

The initial panel tests are in place; next I’m adding `view` specs for grid generation, coordinate/path formatting, dirty tracking, and blocked-node overlay cleanup. After that I’ll add `main` boot-order and CSS regression tests.
I’ve added all four new spec files and will run the visual specs first to confirm they fail as expected before patching implementation.

item.text: I’m fixing the test harness issues caused by VM realm objects and tightening the IDA heuristic assertion so it actually detects the selector bug. After that I’ll rerun the visual specs to capture only real product failures.

item.text: Most tests are now green; one panel test still fails, so I’m isolating that case to inspect the exact runtime values returned by `Panel.getFinder()` for IDA* and adjust either test harness or implementation accordingly.

item.text: I found the root cause: my first patch updated the wrong case block. I’m correcting both selectors explicitly so Jump Point and IDA* each read their own heuristic radio group.

item.text:
Implemented the scenario-builder assignment with TDD and full pass.

### What I added
- New visual test suites:
  - [test/visual/controller.spec.js](/home/sagar/PathFinding.js-fork/test/visual/controller.spec.js)
  - [test/visual/panel.spec.js](/home/sagar/PathFinding.js-fork/test/visual/panel.spec.js)
  - [test/visual/view.spec.js](/home/sagar/PathFinding.js-fork/test/visual/view.spec.js)
  - [test/visual/main-and-dom-integration.spec.js](/home/sagar/PathFinding.js-fork/test/visual/main-and-dom-integration.spec.js)

### Fixes made after failing tests
- IDA* heuristic selector regression fixed in [visual/js/panel.js](/home/sagar/PathFinding.js-fork/visual/js/panel.js):157  
  - Changed IDA* heuristic lookup from `jump_point_heuristic` to `ida_heuristic`.
- Hover selector typo fixed in [visual/css/style.css](/home/sagar/PathFinding.js-fork/visual/css/style.css):59  
  - Changed `#hide_instruction:hover` to `#hide_instructions:hover`.

### Verification
Ran required command successfully:
- `npx mocha --require should test/**/*.js`
- Result: **113 passing, 0 failing**.
